title: GPSR fallback uses expired gradient entries for coordinates
description:
File: python/src/lichen/routing/router.py:480-484

When LOADng is not configured, GPSR fallback looks up gradient entries WITHOUT the 'now' parameter:

```python
dst_entry = self.gradient_table.lookup(dst)  # may be expired but has coords
if dst_entry is not None and dst_entry.coords is not None:
    next_hop = self.gpsr_forward(dst_entry.coords)
```

The comment acknowledges this returns potentially expired entries.

WHY IT'S A BUG: Using coordinates from an expired entry means routing based on stale location data. In a mobile mesh, a node could have moved significantly since the entry expired. GPSR would forward toward the node's old position, causing packets to be routed to the wrong location.

IMPACT: Misdirected packets in mobile deployments when GPSR fallback is used. Security concern: a compromised node could advertise false coordinates, then let the entry expire while GPSR continues routing based on those coordinates.

FIX: Either reject expired coords entirely, or add a separate 'coord_expires' field that allows a grace period for location data distinct from route freshness.
